note: Created after SoC/Gemma4 pivot. Goal narrowed to heterogeneous compute training on RedMagic 10 Pro using a custom Gemma 4 static/megakernel runtime.

Report-only RunPod setup gate collected repo, Python, QAIRT/QNN, Gemma cache, and compile-plan artifacts. Hard blocker: remote clone lacks the active PRD and runbook, so no compile/download/phone command was run.

next_gate: one real-pretrained-weight Gemma layer forward-only on RedMagic Adreno through Vulkan/OpenCL, host PyTorch parity p50 >= 0.99
note: Control plane corrected to prevent drift into RunPod setup theater, Qwen revival, corpus expansion, Reflex implementation, throughput/thermal benchmarking, or training claims before the single-layer forward phone parity gate passes.
Only drift-control text artifacts were synced to RunPod. No model weights, binaries, datasets, SDK artifacts, compiles, downloads, or phone workloads were executed.

H0 single-layer forward phone parity gate is now passed and becomes a regression gate. Result: google/gemma-4-E4B layer 0, OpenCL on REDMAGIC NX789J/SM8750, p50 0.9999890020383452, min 0.9999801999737985, 0 failed tokens, repeat output byte-identical. No fused megakernel/training/backward/Vulkan/NPU/thermal claim is promoted.

Replaced concept PRD with long-horizon overnight execution PRD. Top-level PRD now points to the Gemma4 native phone training authority route. PRD mandates GPD project initialization/repair, gate ladder G0-G10, phone-native HF streaming/tokenization/packing/training, GitHub pivot branch, dependency hygiene, coding commandments, and Resistance V2 anti-demo doctrine.

Installed GPD CLI exposes validation/state/init primitives but not a one-shot project writer; machine contract was validated through GPD and persisted, then formal GPD artifacts were created from the authority PRD.

Imported policy-approved Gemma4 Kernel source/evidence into Polymath, generated import file index, passed Mac CMake build, CTest 2/2, comparator unit tests 3/3, and leak scans. Initial ADB probe succeeded but later ADB/USB transport disappeared; G3 phone execution is blocked until REDMAGIC transport is restored.

Operator reconnected REDMAGIC. ADB rediscovered FY25013101C8. Live G1 OpenCL replay produced sha256 cef523f674cff7ecd01cb59040048f9188f80bcb58b9fc47f1fa7f370ce332cf, matching the passed regression floor.

Ran two sequential google/gemma-4-E4B decoder layers [0,1] on REDMAGIC OpenCL. Layer 1 consumed phone-computed layer 0 output in-process. Compared final layer output to RunPod PyTorch layer-1 reference: p50 0.999993563915067, min 0.9999842650888192, failed tokens 0, max RSS 818764 KB. G1 layer0 hash remained cef523f674cff7ecd01cb59040048f9188f80bcb58b9fc47f1fa7f370ce332cf. No backward/optimizer/training claim promoted.
Added minimal executor interfaces and concrete OpenCL adapter training executor. Latest runner reproduced G1 and G3 output hashes byte-identically.
Ran phone OpenCL rank-4 post-layer0 residual adapter backward. Compared gradients to RunPod PyTorch autograd: cosine_min 0.9999999999999384, failed tensors 0, finite loss 1.6367198658.
Ran phone-side SGD update for rank-4 adapter. Updated adapter tensors matched RunPod reference, trainable hashes changed, frozen layer0/layer1 safetensors hashes stayed stable.

Phone used system curl to fetch CC0 Hugging Face text, native C++ Gemma BPE tokenizer, and UFS cache packing. RunPod Transformers oracle reported zero input ID and attention mask mismatches for 3 seq128 rows.

Rejected G8 because the current training step still consumes hidden-state fixtures; next valid repair is phone-native token IDs to layer_input and per_layer_input generation from frozen Gemma embedding/PLE assets.

Phone runtime generated Gemma layer input and layer0/1 PLE tensors from phone token cache plus immutable Gemma assets, ran OpenCL layer0/layer1, applied rank-4 adapter SGD, and passed RunPod PyTorch comparisons plus G1/G3 regressions.
Predeclared three-batch phone-native training chain passed with checkpoint chaining, per-batch RunPod oracle comparisons, thermal/storage telemetry, and post-chain G1/G3 regressions.
Final falsifier review passed under narrow rank-4 two-layer distillation adapter scope; no full Gemma4, Hexagon NPU, six-hour endurance, benchmark, or theoretical-maximum claim promoted.
Used stored Hugging Face auth without printing token, fetched corpus on phone, ran phone --run-g8-distill, and established token bridge timing baseline at 4.232976s.

Projected PLE rows cached per token ID. Phone A/B improved token-to-hidden from 4.232976s to 0.667287s (6.343561316195281x) with token, bridge, layer, adapter, checkpoint, and artifact-hygiene gates passing.
